title: hieracium leiopogon subsp. coquozianum
intro: Hieracium leiopogon subsp. coquozianum, also known as Coquoz's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and a rosette of leaves. The medicinal properties of Coquoz's Hawkweed include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is known to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Coquoz's Hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
